mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack eliminates wage inflation pressures and forces central bank easing expectations, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand and housing wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply chain disruption and earnings recession fears, triggering rapid portfolio de-risking and negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of instances following a greater than 0.5 percent INDPRO decline during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with stagnant policy rates compress real yields and delay tightening cycles, which supports equity valuations and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% MoM while FEDFUNDS remains flat or rises
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of historical occurrences across all recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations over a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent annualized confirms economic stagnation and deteriorating corporate earnings trajectories, triggering immediate market de-risking and negative five-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates capacity utilization tightening, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns during inflationary periods.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.4% MoM while UNRATE falls > 0.2pp
failure_condition: The divergence condition produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases during periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0 percent.
